name: Brenda Fassie
description: '''Brenda Nokuzola Fassie''' (3 November 1964 – 9 May 2004) was a South African singer, songwriter, dancer and activist. Affectionately called '''MaBrrr''' by her fans, she is also known as the "Queen of African Pop" or the "[[Madonna]] of the [[Township (South Africa)|Townships]]". Fassie was a legendary figure in the South African music industry, celebrated for her powerful voice, captivating stage presence, and commitment to social justice, often called one of the most influential and greatest musicians on the African Continent. She produced songs that contributed to the influence of music genres such as [[Pop music|pop]] and [[kwaito]], with some of her work addressing social issues in South Africa. Despite her outrageous and controversial stage presence, her name, Nokuzola, means "quiet", "calm", or "peace".
